sections.label: Section 497
sections.translation: Either send Justice to battle for those dear to you , or grant us in turn to get a similar grip [*] on them , if indeed after defeat you would in turn win victory .

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: καὶ τῆσδ ἄκουσον λοισθίου βοῆς , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: So listen , father , to this last appeal of mine as you behold these fledglings crouching at your tomb . Have compassion on your offspring , on the woman and on the man as well , and let not this seed of Pelops ’ line be blotted out : for then , in spite of death , you are not dead .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.translation: For children are voices of salvation to a man , though he is dead ; like corks , they buoy up the net , saving the flaxen cord from out of the deep . Hear ! For your own sake we make this lament . By honoring this plea of ours you save yourself .

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἀμεμφῆ τόνδ [*] ἐτείνατον λόγον ,
sections.translation: In truth you have drawn out this plea of yours to your own content in showing honor to this unlamented tomb . As for the rest , since your heart is rightly set on action , put your fortune to the test and get to your work at once .

sections.label: Section 514
sections.original: ἔσται : πυθέσθαι δ οὐδέν ἐστ ἔξω δρόμου ,
sections.translation: It will be so . But it is in no way amiss to inquire how , from what motive ,

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: πόθεν χοὰς ἔπεμψεν , ἐκ τίνος λόγου
sections.translation: she came to send her libations , seeking too late to make amends for an irremediable deed . They would be a sorry gift to send to the senseless dead : I cannot guess what they mean . The gifts are too paltry for her offence .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: τὰ πάντα γάρ τις ἐκχέας ἀνθ αἵματος
sections.translation: For though a man may pour out all he has in atonement for one deed of blood , it is wasted effort . So the saying goes . If indeed you know , tell me : I wish to learn .

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original: οἶδ , ὦ τέκνον , παρῆ [*] γάρ : ἔκ τ ὀνειράτων
sections.translation: I know , my child , for I was there . It was because she was shaken by dreams and wandering terrors of the night

sections.label: Section 525
sections.translation: that she sent these offerings , godless woman that she is .

sections.label: Section 526
sections.original: ἦ καὶ πέπυσθε τοὔναρ , ὥστ ὀρθῶς φράσαι ;
sections.translation: And have you learned the nature of the dream so as to tell it properly ?

sections.label: Section 527
sections.original: τεκεῖν δράκοντ ἔδοξεν , ὡς αὐτὴ λέγει .
sections.translation: She dreamed she gave birth to a serpent : that is her own account .

sections.label: Section 528
sections.original: καὶ ποῖ τελευτᾷ καὶ καρανοῦται λόγος ;
sections.translation: And where does the tale end , and what is its consummation ?

sections.label: Section 529
sections.translation: She laid it to rest as if it were a child , in swaddling clothes .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.translation: What food did it crave , the newborn viper ?

sections.label: Section 531
sections.translation: In her dream she offered it her own breast .

sections.label: Section 532
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ἄτρωτον οὖθαρ ἦν [*] ὑπὸ στύγους ; [*]
sections.translation: Surely her nipple was not unwounded by the loathsome beast ?

sections.label: Section 533
sections.original: ὥστ ἐν γάλακτι θρόμβον αἵματος σπάσαι .
sections.translation: No : it drew in clotted blood with the milk .

sections.label: Section 534
sections.translation: Truly it is not without meaning : the vision signifies a man !

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: ἡ δ ἐξ ὕπνου κέκλαγγεν [*] ἐπτοημένη .
sections.translation: Then from out of her sleep she raised a shriek and awoke appalled , and many lamps that had been blinded in the darkness flared up in the house to cheer our mistress . Then she sent these libations for the dead in the hope that they might be an effective cure for her distress .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: ἀλλ εὔχομαι γῇ τῇδε καὶ πατρὸς τάφῳ
sections.translation: Well then , I pray to this earth and to my father ’ s grave that this dream may come to its fulfilment in me . As I understand it , it fits at every point . For if the snake left the same place as I ; if it was furnished with my swaddling clothes ;

sections.label: Section 545
sections.translation: if it sought to open its mouth to take the breast that nourished me and mixed the sweet milk with clotted blood while she shrieked for terror at this , then surely , as she has nourished a portentous thing of horror , she must die by violence . For I , turned serpent ,

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: κτείνω νιν , ὡς τοὔνειρον ἐννέπει τόδε .
sections.translation: am her killer , as this dream declares .

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: τερασκόπον δὴ [*] τῶνδέ σ αἱροῦμαι πέρι .
sections.translation: I choose your reading of this portent . Let it be so . As for the rest , give your friends their parts . Tell some what to do , others what to leave undone .

sections.label: Section 554
sections.original: ἁπλοῦς ὁ μῦθος : τήνδε μὲν στείχειν [*] ἔσω ,
sections.translation: It is a simple story . My sister must go inside , and I charge her to keep concealed this pact with me ,

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: αἰνῶ δὲ κρύπτειν τάσδε συνθήκας ἐμάς ,
sections.translation: so that as by craft they killed a worthy man , so by craft they may likewise be caught and perish in the very same snare , even as Loxias decreed , lord Apollo , the prophet who has never before been false .

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ξένῳ γὰρ εἰκώς , παντελῆ σαγὴν ἔχων ,
sections.translation: In the guise of a stranger , one fully equipped , I will come to the outer gate , and with me Pylades , whom you see here , as a guest and ally of the house . Both of us will speak the speech of Parnassus , imitating the accent of a Phocian tongue . And in case none of the keepers of the door will give us a hearty welcome

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: καὶ δὴ θυρωρῶν οὔτις ἂν φαιδρᾷ φρενὶ
sections.translation: on the plea that the house is afflicted with trouble by the gods , then we will wait so that anyone passing the house will consider and say : Why then does Aegisthus have his door shut on his suppliant , if in fact he is at home and knows ?

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: εἰ δ οὖν ἀμείψω βαλὸν [*] ἑρκείων [*] πυλῶν
sections.translation: But if I indeed pass the outermost threshold of the gate and find that man sitting on my father ’ s throne , or if then coming face to face with me he lifts and casts down his eyes , know well :

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: πρὶν αὐτὸν εἰπεῖν “ ποδαπὸς ὁ ξένος ;” νεκρὸν
sections.translation:
before he can even say Of what land is this stranger ? I will skewer him with my swift sword and lay him dead . The fury that has no fill of slaughter shall for her third and crowning drink drink unmixed blood !
Now you , Electra , keep strict watch over what happens inside the house ,

sections.label: Section 580
sections.translation: so that our plans may fit together well . You addressing the Chorus had best keep a discreet tongue : be silent when there is need and speak only what the occasion demands . As for the rest , I call on him [*] to cast his glance this way and direct the contest of the sword for me . Exeunt Orestes , Pylades , and Electra

sections.label: Section 1.585
sections.translation: Many are the horrors , dread and appalling , bred of earth , and the arms of the deep teem with hateful monsters . Likewise between heaven and earth

sections.label: Section 1.590
sections.translation: lights [*] hung high in the air draw near ; and winged things and things that walk the earth can also tell of the stormy wrath of whirlwinds .

sections.label: Section 1.594
sections.translation: But who can tell of man ’ s overweening spirit ,

sections.label: Section 1.595
sections.translation: and of the reckless passions of women hardened of soul , partners of the woes of mortals ? Inordinate passion , overmastering the female ,

sections.label: Section 1.600
sections.translation: gains a fatal victory over the wedded unions of beasts and humans alike .

sections.label: Section 2.602
sections.translation: Let whoever is not flighty in his wits know this , when he has learned of the device of a lit brand contrived

sections.label: Section 2.605
sections.translation: by Thestius ’ heartless daughter : [*] she destroyed her own child by burning the charred brand of the same age as he when , coming from his mother ’ s womb , he cried out ,

sections.label: Section 2.610
sections.translation: and it aged in pace with him through his life to the day decreed by fate .

sections.label: Section 2.612
sections.original: ἄλλαν δ ἔστιν [*] ἐν λόγοις στυγεῖν
sections.translation: And there is in legend another murderous virgin to be loathed , [*]

sections.label: Section 2.615
sections.translation: who ruined a loved one at the bidding of his foes , when , lured by Minos ’ gift , the Cretan necklace forged of gold , she with her dog ’ s heart despoiled Nisus of his immortal lock

sections.label: Section 2.620
sections.translation: as he drew breath in unsuspecting sleep . And Hermes [*] overtook him .

sections.label: Section 3.623
sections.translation: But since I have recalled tales of pitiless afflictions , it is the right time to tell of a marriage void of love ,

sections.label: Section 3.625
sections.translation: an abomination to the house , and the plots devised by a wife ’ s cunning against her warrior lord , against her lord revered with reason by his foes . But I honor the hearths of homes not heated by passion ’ s fires ,

sections.label: Section 3.630
sections.translation: and in woman a spirit that shrinks from audacious deeds .

sections.label: Section 3.631
sections.translation: Indeed the Lemnian [*] holds first place among evils in story : it has long been told with groans as an abominable calamity . Men compare each new horror to Lemnian troubles ;

sections.label: Section 3.635
sections.translation: and because of a woeful deed abhorred by the gods a race has disappeared , cast out in infamy from among mortals . For no man reveres what is hated by the gods . Is there one of these tales I have gathered that I do not justly cite ?

sections.label: Section 4.639
sections.translation: But the keen and bitter sword is near the breast and drives home its blow at the bidding of Justice .

sections.label: Section 4.640
sections.translation: For truly the injustice of him who has unjustly transgressed the sovereign majesty of Zeus

sections.label: Section 4.645
sections.translation: lies on the ground trampled under foot . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.646
sections.translation: The anvil of Justice is planted firm . Destiny fashions her arms and forges her sword quickly , and the famed and deeply brooding Fury is bringing the son into our house ,

sections.label: Section 4.650
sections.translation: to requite at last the pollution of blood shed long ago .

sections.label: Section 652
sections.original: παῖ παῖ , θύρας ἄκουσον ἑρκείας κτύπον .
sections.translation: Boy ! Boy ! Hear my knocking at the outer door ! Who is inside ? Boy ! Boy ! I say again , who is at home ?

sections.label: Section 655
sections.translation: Again for the third time I call for some one to come out of the house , if by Aegisthus ’ will it offers welcome to strangers .

sections.label: Section 657
sections.original: εἶεν , ἀκούω : ποδαπὸς ὁ ξένος ; πόθεν ;
sections.translation: Yes , yes , I hear . Of what land is the stranger , and whence ?

sections.label: Section 658
sections.translation: Announce me to the masters of the house , for it is in fact to them that I come bearing news .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: τάχυνε δ , ὡς καὶ νυκτὸς ἅρμ ἐπείγεται
sections.translation: And hurry , since the chariot of night is speeding on with darkness , and it is time for wayfarers to drop anchor in some house friendly to all guests . Tell some one to come forth who has authority over the house , the mistress in charge . But the master would be more fitting ,

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: αἰδὼς γὰρ ἐν λεχθεῖσιν οὐκ ἐπαργέμους
sections.translation: for then no delicacy in speaking makes words obscure : man speaks boldly to man and reveals his meaning without reserve . The Servant withdraws . Clytaemestra appears at the door with a maid-servant in attendance

sections.label: Section 668
sections.original: ξένοι , λέγοιτ ἂν εἴ τι δεῖ : πάρεστι γὰρ
sections.translation: Strangers , you have only to declare your need , for we have everything that suits this house : warm baths , beds to charm away fatigue ,

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: καὶ θερμὰ λουτρὰ καὶ πόνων θελκτήριος [*]
sections.translation: and the presence of honest faces . But if there is another matter requiring graver counsel , that is the concern of men , and we will communicate with them .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: ξένος μέν εἰμι Δαυλιεὺς ἐκ Φωκέων :
sections.translation: I am a stranger , a Daulian of the Phocians . As I was on my way , carrying my pack on business of my own to Argos ,
